entities.name: FERTIGATION
entities.description: The application of plant nutrients through irrigation systems directly to the root zone.

entities.name: VEGETABLE CROPS
entities.description: Crops grown for food, specifically vegetables, used as the subject for fertigation application.

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of crop output and yield resulting from nutrient and water management.

entities.name: RESOURCE USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which resources like water and nutrients are utilized in crop production.

entities.name: PLANT NUTRIENTS
entities.description: Essential chemical elements required for plant growth and development, delivered via fertigation.

entities.name: NUTRIENT USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which plants absorb and utilize applied nutrients, often abbreviated as NUE.

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A critical plant nutrient, specifically mentioned as nitrate NO3, prone to leaching.

entities.name: NUTRIENT LEACHING
entities.description: The process where water-soluble nutrients are washed out of the root zone, which fertigation helps control.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The geographical region where the adoption of fertigation in practical agriculture is discussed.
